body:
### Security objective

This control ensures that **privileged users and administrators authenticate with MFA or 2-step verification**.

### Privileged identities reviewed

The assessment reviews administrative users and privileged assignments across AWS, GCP, Azure, Microsoft Entra, Alibaba Cloud, Okta, and Google Workspace. It focuses on identities that can administer cloud resources, identity settings, or security-relevant applications without MFA or 2SV.

### Security impact

Administrator accounts are high-value targets. Without MFA, a single compromised password may be enough to access consoles, APIs, identity policy, workloads, data stores, and security controls.

Privileged access should require MFA before elevation or sign-in. Standing administrator access should be minimized through just-in-time access, privileged identity management, role-based federation, and group-based assignment.
name: Ensure administrators have multi-factor authentication enabled
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Enable MFA or 2-step verification for every privileged user, then enforce it through the identity platform so administrative access cannot bypass MFA. This control identifies admin users or privileged assignments where MFA/2SV is not active.

### AWS

1. Open **IAM > Users** and select the affected administrative user.
2. Enable a virtual, hardware, or passkey MFA device under **Security credentials**.
3. Remove unused AdministratorAccess, *FullAccess, or equivalent policies if the user does not need them.
4. Prefer IAM Identity Center and role-based federation over long-lived administrator IAM users.
5. Validate MFA enrollment:

~~~bash
aws iam list-mfa-devices --user-name {{asset.name}}
aws iam list-attached-user-policies --user-name {{asset.name}}
aws iam list-groups-for-user --user-name {{asset.name}}
~~~

References:
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_mfa_enable_virtual.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/singlesignon/latest/userguide/mfa-enable-how-to.html

The control is satisfied when AWS administrator-style IAM users have MFA device data or no longer hold administrative access.

### GCP

1. Identify the affected Google Cloud administrator account and the IAM role binding that grants privileged access.
2. In Google Admin, enforce 2-Step Verification for the account, group, or organizational unit.
3. Remove roles/owner, roles/editor, or broad admin role bindings if they are no longer required.
4. Prefer group-based privileged access with 2SV enforcement over direct user bindings.
5. Validate IAM bindings:

~~~bash
gcloud projects get-iam-policy <project-id> \
  --flatten="bindings[].members" \
  --filter="bindings.members:<user-email>"
~~~

References:
- https://support.google.com/a/answer/9176657
- https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/using-iam-securely

The control is satisfied when Google Cloud admin users are enrolled in 2SV or no longer have privileged IAM roles.

### Azure

1. Identify the affected Microsoft Entra user, admin role, or Azure privileged role assignment.
2. Require MFA through Conditional Access or Security Defaults.
3. Include privileged Azure roles such as Owner, Contributor, classic administrator assignments, and Entra admin roles.
4. Use Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management for just-in-time privileged access where available.
5. Validate role assignments and MFA enforcement:

~~~bash
az role assignment list --assignee <user-object-id> --all
~~~

References:
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/tutorial-enable-azure-mfa
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/howto-conditional-access-policy-admin-mfa
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/id-governance/privileged-identity-management/pim-configure

The control is satisfied when privileged Entra/Azure users have MFA active or the privileged assignment is removed.

### Alibaba

1. Open **RAM > Users** and select the affected administrator user.
2. Bind an MFA device to the RAM user.
3. Remove AdministratorAccess, *FullAccess, or equivalent RAM policies if the user does not need them.
4. Prefer role-based federation for administrators where possible.
5. Validate MFA and policies:

~~~bash
aliyun ram GetUser --UserName <user-name>
aliyun ram ListPoliciesForUser --UserName <user-name>
aliyun ram ListGroupsForUser --UserName <user-name>
~~~

Reference: https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/en/ram/user-guide/enable-an-mfa-device-for-a-ram-user

The control is satisfied when Alibaba administrator RAM users have MFA bound or no longer hold administrative permissions.

### Okta

1. Open **Directory > People** and select the affected Okta administrator.
2. Enroll the user in an approved MFA factor.
3. Open **Security > Authentication Policies** or the Admin Console sign-on policy.
4. Require phishing-resistant or approved MFA for Okta Admin Console access.
5. Remove administrator roles that are no longer required.

References:
- https://help.okta.com/oie/en-us/content/topics/identity-engine/policies/about-authentication-policies.htm
- https://help.okta.com/oie/en-us/content/topics/security/mfa/mfa.htm

The control is satisfied when Okta admins are covered by an MFA-enabled Admin Console policy.

### Google Workspace

1. Open **Admin Console > Directory > Users** and select the affected admin user.
2. Ensure the user is enrolled in 2-Step Verification.
3. Open **Security > Authentication > 2-step verification** and enforce 2SV for administrators or their organizational unit.
4. Remove super admin or delegated admin roles that are no longer needed.
5. Prefer security keys for highly privileged accounts.

References:
- https://support.google.com/a/answer/9176657
- https://support.google.com/a/answer/7576830

The control is satisfied when Google Workspace administrator users are enrolled in 2SV or no longer hold admin roles.
description: Administrative users or privileged assignments without MFA or 2-step verification enabled.
